message:
fix(connection): clear direct-INSERT append hint on rollback paths too

Follow-up to a6086f5c, which cleared
prepared_direct_insert_append_hint on the autocommit-commit close
paths. The same retained leaf image must not survive rollback
either: a rollback returns the database to a state that may not
contain the page the hint points at, and a subsequent
prepared-statement execution would then chase a stale leaf
pointer or trip the duplicate-pointer assertion.

Two more close sites identified by tracing every path that flips
`concurrent_txn.set(false)`:

1) Implicit-rollback path (autocommit boundary): after the rollback
   tries-and-fails path, before reload_memdb_from_pager runs, clear
   the hint so the post-reload state cannot reuse a leaf pointer
   that pre-dates the reload.

2) Retained-rollback inner branch: same site, inside the variant
   that ran with retained-autocommit accounting. The
   txn_metrics_mark_finished() landmark stays the canonical "this
   transaction is gone" fence; the clear runs immediately after.

Collapse a one-time multi-line `assert_eq!` formatting to a single
line. Cosmetic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
